_comment: Expected REACHABLE findings — static + dynamic correlation produces single CRITICAL verdict

finding.title: Confirmed supply chain malware: credential theft + exfiltration
finding.description: Package muaddib-simulation executes malicious code during pip install via cmdclass override. Confirmed credential theft (honeypot access) and exfiltration attempt (outbound network) in sandbox. Static analysis corroborates: exec(base64.b64decode(...)) pattern, hardcoded C2 endpoints, sensitive file path access.
finding.recommendation: BLOCK INSTALLATION — Do not use this package.

attack_chain.trigger: pip install (automatic — no user action needed)
attack_chain.stage_3: harvest_env_vars() — reads AWS_*, GITHUB_*, NPM_TOKEN, SECRET_KEY
attack_chain.stage_4: exfiltrate() — POST to c2.muaddib-attack.test + DNS fallback

correlated_signals.description: Code injection via exec() with decoded input
Credential files accessed during install (6 honeypots triggered)
Sensitive environment variables accessed during install
Credential read + outbound network attempt = confirmed exfiltration chain

comparison.semgrep_alone: 4 warnings — no actionable verdict, high FP rate
comparison.guarddog_alone: 3 alerts — suspicious but not confirmed, cmdclass has high FP
comparison.reachable: 1 CRITICAL — confirmed malware with behavioral proof from sandbox

notes: Static signals alone cannot confirm malware (cmdclass and exec are used by legitimate packages)
Sandbox provides behavioral proof: credential theft + exfiltration chain
The key differentiator: REACHABLE correlates what the code LOOKS like (static) with what it DOES (dynamic)
Noise reduction: 8 fragmented signals → 1 high-confidence correlated finding
This is the Python equivalent of Shai-Hulud (npm) — same intent, different entry mechanism
